summary: Recent changes to the autoplay functionality in Firefox focused on improving memory management related to media elements. Specifically, by adding missing cycle collection entries in the media domain, the browser can now better manage and free up memory from resources that are no longer needed. This is important for ensuring that media playback runs smoothly without unnecessary memory consumption, which can enhance overall browser performance and user experience when interacting with autoplay videos or audio. _While users may not notice immediate changes_, these enhancements help Firefox remain efficient, especially when handling multiple media sources.
